title: Extract a bloated LiveView into LiveView + LiveComponent + function components
prompt: Refactor test_fixtures/multi_antipattern_liveview.ex into a clean 3-layer architecture. Output three files: (1) lib/my_app_web/live/project_board_live.ex (MyAppWeb.ProjectBoardLive, Phoenix.LiveView) — handles mount, handle_params, handle_info for real-time updates, and top-level state. (2) lib/my_app_web/live/project_board_live/task_card.ex (MyAppWeb.ProjectBoardLive.TaskCard, Phoenix.LiveComponent) — stateful per-task state, handles its own phx-click events for status changes. (3) lib/my_app_web/components/project_board_header.ex (MyAppWeb.Components.ProjectBoardHeader) — stateless function component with attr declarations for the page header. The LiveView must NOT contain DB calls in mount, must use streams for tasks, and must use connected?/1 before subscribing to "project:#{@project_id}". The LiveComponent handles its own phx-click="mark_done" and updates state via send_update. The function component uses attr declarations for :title and :user_count.
tier_rationale: The broadest refactor in the family. Touches 5 capabilities: architectural decomposition, anti-pattern removal, LiveComponent extraction, function component extraction, stream conversion. Single challenge spans 3 files. Sonnet rarely gets the full 3-layer decomposition right — typically either leaves too much in the LiveView or extracts to flat helper modules.
